# Beudox HR Management System - Technical Context

## Architecture Overview

This is a modern React-based HR management application built with TypeScript, using Vite as the build tool. The application follows a component-based architecture with clear separation between UI components, business logic, and data management.

## Key Technologies & Patterns

### Frontend Framework
- **React 18** with functional components and hooks
- **TypeScript** for type safety
- **Vite** for fast development and optimized builds

### UI & Styling
- **shadcn/ui** component library built on Radix UI primitives
- **Tailwind CSS** for utility-first styling
- **Lucide React** for consistent iconography
- Responsive design with mobile-first approach

### State Management & Data Fetching
- **TanStack Query** (React Query) for server state management
- **React Hook Form** with Zod validation for form handling
- Local component state with React hooks

### Routing & Navigation
- **React Router DOM** for client-side routing
- Sidebar-based navigation with collapsible sections
- Route-based page titles in the top bar

### Backend Integration
- **Supabase** as the backend-as-a-service platform
- PostgreSQL database with real-time capabilities
- Supabase Auth for user authentication
- Row Level Security (RLS) for data access control

## Core Components & Structure

### Layout System
- `AppLayout`: Main application layout with sidebar and content area
- `AppSidebar`: Collapsible sidebar with navigation sections
- `TopBar`: Header with dynamic page titles

### Navigation Structure
The sidebar is organized into logical sections:
- **MAIN**: Dashboard
- **PEOPLE**: Employees, Attendance, Holidays, Leave Management
- **FINANCE**: Payroll, Finance Sheet, Loans, Expenses, Outsourcing
- **WORK**: Projects, Evaluations, HR Policies
- **SYSTEM**: Notifications, Settings

### Key Files & Their Purposes

#### Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx`: Application bootstrap, renders the root App component
- `src/App.tsx`: Main app component with routing setup
- `src/pages/Index.tsx`: Placeholder home page (needs implementation)

#### Layout Components
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx`: Main layout wrapper
- `src/components/layout/AppSidebar.tsx`: Navigation sidebar with user info
- `src/components/layout/TopBar.tsx`: Page header with titles

#### UI Components
- `src/components/BeudoxLogo.tsx`: Logo component with variant support
- `src/components/NavLink.tsx`: Enhanced NavLink with active state styling
- `src/components/ui/`: shadcn/ui component library (40+ components)

#### Integrations
- `src/integrations/supabase/`: Supabase client setup and types
- `src/hooks/useAuth.ts`: Authentication hook
- `src/hooks/use-toast.ts`: Toast notification system

#### Configuration
- `package.json`: Dependencies and scripts
- `vite.config.ts`: Vite build configuration
- `tailwind.config.ts`: Tailwind CSS configuration
- `tsconfig.json`: TypeScript configuration
- `.env`: Environment variables for Supabase

## Database Schema & Relationships

The application uses Supabase with PostgreSQL. Key tables include:
- `employees`: Employee profiles and information
- `attendance`: Time tracking and attendance records
- `leave_requests`: Vacation and leave management
- `payroll`: Salary and compensation data
- `projects`: Project management and assignments
- `evaluations`: Performance reviews
- `notifications`: System notifications

## Business Logic & Data Flow

### Authentication Flow
- Users authenticate via Supabase Auth
- Employee data is fetched and stored in context
- Route protection based on authentication status

### Data Management
- CRUD operations handled through Supabase client
- Optimistic updates with TanStack Query
- Real-time subscriptions for live data updates

### Form Handling
- React Hook Form for form state management
- Zod schemas for validation
- Error handling and user feedback

## Development Workflow

### Scripts
- `npm run dev`: Development server with hot reload
- `npm run build`: Production build optimization
- `npm run test`: Unit testing with Vitest
- `npm run lint`: Code linting with ESLint

### Testing Strategy
- Unit tests for components and utilities
- E2E tests with Playwright for critical user flows
- Component testing with React Testing Library

## Deployment & Environment

- **Development**: Local development with Vite dev server
- **Production**: Built with Vite and deployed to static hosting
- **Environment Variables**: Supabase configuration via .env files
- **CI/CD**: Configurable build and deployment pipelines

## Security Considerations

- Supabase RLS policies for data access control
- Environment variable management for secrets
- Input validation with Zod schemas
- Secure authentication flows

## Performance Optimizations

- Vite's fast build times and HMR
- Tree shaking and code splitting
- Optimized bundle sizes with modern build tools
- Efficient re-renders with React best practices

## Future Enhancements

- Implement actual page components beyond the placeholder
- Add more comprehensive error boundaries
- Implement caching strategies for better performance
- Add internationalization support
- Enhance accessibility features
